tasks.title: Address context-rotting static files
tasks.notes: decisions.md, session-history.md, pickup.md rot between sessions — replace with maintainable alternatives. EXTENDED: The vault project view currently stores static agent-written files (project-state.md, decisions.md, session-history.md, pickup.md). These should NOT be stored documents — they should be generated on-demand from live sources of truth: tasks.json, git log, watcher.db, session branches. Solution: GET /api/project/{name}/context endpoint that assembles a fresh snapshot at query time. Permanent artifacts (case studies, design docs, real work products) stay as files. System state summaries are never stored — always generated.

tasks.title: Evaluate OpenClaw for Tier 2 watcher agents
tasks.notes: Persistent intelligent agents for session-history and decisions updates

tasks.title: Explore .claude directory for vault integration
tasks.notes: Investigate transcript files, memories, settings, and other .claude contents. Determine what can be incorporated into the vault system for better context persistence.

tasks.title: Research MCP servers for vault system
tasks.notes: Look into MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers — could provide structured tool access to vault operations, task tracking, and context loading for any Claude interface.

tasks.title: Research sub-agents for vault automation
tasks.notes: Investigate Claude Code sub-agents and Agent SDK for automating vault operations — potential path for Tier 2 watcher agents, context maintenance, and multi-step workflows.

tasks.title: Track chat sessions per project (transcript linkage)
tasks.notes: Track which Claude sessions interacted with each project. Link to specific transcript .jsonl files. Need to solve auto-detection of current session ID. Design: per-project sessions.json or extend event log.

tasks.title: Add provenance tracking to tasks (transcript + message linkage)
tasks.notes: When tasks are created or modified, record which transcript file and ideally which message triggered the change. This creates a traceable chain from task back to the conversation that spawned it. Could store as metadata on each task entry (created_from, modified_from fields) pointing to transcript:message_id pairs.

tasks.title: Transform ML OS demo into agent-facing context pack builder
tasks.notes: CLI-first, not UI-first. Any agent should be able to compose context packs via commands — select kernel, schema, scenario, attach vault sources, stamp with provenance, output a deployable seed prompt. The web UI is secondary. Priority: (1) CLI compose toolchain (2) API endpoints for remote agents (3) Optional web composer. The created agent's context pack should reference the transcript/session where it was designed. Ties to t-fa0b53 (provenance) and t-b7e972 (session tracking).

tasks.title: Design context pack generator — grounding prompt factory for domain-specific agent environments
tasks.notes: Based on ML OS Case Study 001 (Net+ Autonomous Grounding). Generator takes: domain description, intellectual history, canonical state, operator profile, identifiable gap. Outputs: complete .context/ folder with mimir.md, workspace.md, engine file with Phase 0, CLAUDE.md boot sequence. Reference: Runtime Grounding Sequence protocol (Identity Recall > State Ingestion > History Loading > Artifact Contextualization > Gap Identification). Added by cloud agent (Claude Chat) session 2026-03-07.

tasks.title: Revisit: associate Claude Chat App sessions with vault + transcript capture strategy
tasks.notes: Options explored: manual export, session branch metadata (chat URL stored in .session-meta), Cowork browser automation, manual API ping at session start. Core question: do we need transcript content locally or just the association? Defer until clearer. Reference: GAP-002, GAP-008.

tasks.title: Add created_from + agent_id fields to task schema and ingest.py
tasks.notes: Add created_from (session branch or chat URL) and agent_id (e.g. 'cloud-agent', 'code-agent', 'operator') to tasks.json schema. Update ingest.py task add with optional --session and --agent flags. Backward compatible — existing tasks get null for new fields. Closes GAP-002 partially.

tasks.title: Add manual refresh button to vault browser tasks view
tasks.notes: Temp workaround until direct API sync is implemented. Button on tasks page that re-fetches /api/tasks and updates the view. No page reload needed — just re-query and re-render.

tasks.title: Architect direct cloud-agent-to-vault sync via tunnel (Cloudflare/ngrok)
tasks.notes: Current path: cloud agent → GitHub → git pull → local file → browser. 4 hops, manual. Target: expose localhost:3001 via Cloudflare Tunnel or ngrok, cloud agent writes directly to POST /api/tasks, SSE pushes update to browser instantly. Git reverts to its proper role: version control + session branches only, not sync layer.

tasks.title: Build browser extension for real-time Claude Chat transcript capture
tasks.notes: Real-time DOM observer on claude.ai. MutationObserver watches message container — captures {role, text, timestamp, message_id} on each new message node. Logs deletions as events (append-only event log, not a mirror). Native Messaging Host writes to vault/{project}/.session-transcripts/{chat_id}.jsonl — same .jsonl format as Claude Code transcripts so watcher.py can parse both surfaces. Transcript registered in .session-chats.json with surface: claude-chat. Key design decisions: (1) capture on send/receive only not drafts, (2) deletion = logged event not silent drop, (3) timestamps preserved. Closes the cloud agent context gap — without this, Claude Chat sessions leave no local artifact. Session-association model: transcript lives on the session branch under .session-transcripts/, merged to main when session closes.

tasks.title: Add active file watcher to watcher.py for real-time transcript ingestion
tasks.notes: watcher.py is currently parse-on-demand only — requires manual invocation of 'watcher.py parse <file>'. No active watching loop exists. Fix: add a watchdog daemon mode using Python watchdog library (or polling fallback) that monitors ~/.claude/projects/ for new or modified .jsonl files and auto-parses them into watcher.db as they're written. This is GAP-008. Related: t-9271e6 (browser extension for Claude Chat) is the same problem on a different surface — both should write to the same .jsonl format and downstream pipeline.

tasks.title: Design system for agents to self-modify boot sequences, engines, and CLAUDE.md
tasks.notes: Currently CLAUDE.md, engine files, and boot sequences are manually maintained. Agents notice drift and emergence (flow.md reflexivity protocol) but can't act on it — they can only surface observations to Mimir. Goal: give agents a sanctioned pathway to propose and commit modifications to their own operational context. Design questions: (1) What can an agent modify autonomously vs what requires Mimir approval? (2) How do proposed changes get reviewed before merging — PR model, INBOX proposal, or direct commit with revert option? (3) Which files are in scope: CLAUDE.md, .context/engines/, .context/workspace.md, .context/mimir.md (probably never), .claude/commands/? (4) How does the agent know if its modification worked — what's the feedback loop? (5) How do we prevent drift accumulation where each agent nudges the boot sequence in a slightly different direction? Related: flow.md reflexivity protocol, t-cbeff8 (context rot), GAP-009 (CLAUDE.md files uncoordinated). This is the long-term path to a self-improving workspace.

tasks.title: Figure out what to do with stored Claude Chat transcript URLs
tasks.notes: Two Claude Chat share URLs stored in OpenClaw_Claude/.claude/transcripts/registry.md for session bwgh (2026-03-07). URLs: https://claude.ai/share/ef10710c-f1aa-4035-bc99-f5521c354a13 and https://claude.ai/share/e2be28ae-36c5-47b6-8514-e57cb590b018. Share pages return 403 when fetched programmatically. Questions to resolve: (1) Can Cowork browser agent scrape these while logged in? (2) Should the registry live on the session branch instead of main? (3) What schema should transcript entries follow when t-9271e6 is built — how do URL-based entries migrate to local .jsonl entries? (4) Should INBOX point to registry.md so boot agents know transcripts exist?

tasks.title: Refactor hardcoded paths to use MLOS_ROOT env var for multi-device support
tasks.notes:

Path refactor is COMPLETE — scripts and CLAUDE.md already updated.
This task is now only about the physical folder move and .env setup.

## What has already been done

- CLAUDE.md updated to reference $MLOS_ROOT etc. (cloud agent, commit 324999c, OpenClaw_Claude)
- .env added to .gitignore

## What still needs to happen (after t-6957e3 is complete)

Step 1 — Move folders from Desktop to confirmed Z: target:
  - C:/Users/Erinh/Desktop/Home_Lab_2026   to   Z:/<path>/Home_Lab_2026
  - C:/Users/Erinh/Desktop/OpenClaw_Claude  to   Z:/<path>/OpenClaw_Claude
  - C:/Users/Erinh/Desktop/ClaudeTest       to   Z:/<path>/ClaudeTest
  Use robocopy or file explorer. Confirm with Mimir before moving.

Step 2 — Create .env at Z:/<path>/Home_Lab_2026/.env:

  VAULT_DIR=Z:/<confirmed_path>/Home_Lab_2026/vault

Step 4 — Test on primary device:

## Acceptance criteria

2. .env is NOT in git status (gitignored)
3. No remaining hardcoded Desktop paths anywhere
4. Second device can run bootstrap.sh with its own .env

tasks.title: Audit shared drive for existing git repos before folder migration
tasks.notes:
BLOCKS t-674952 (folder move to shared drive). Run this task first.

## Context
Mimir wants to move Home_Lab_2026, OpenClaw_Claude, and ClaudeTest from C:/Users/Erinh/Desktop/ to shared drive Z: for multi-device access. The shared drive may already have git repos that could collide. Audit Z: before anything is moved.

## Exact commands to run

Step 1 — List all git repos on Z drive:
  In cmd:      dir /s /b Z:\.git 2>nul
  In Git Bash: find /z/ -name ".git" -maxdepth 4 2>/dev/null
  Paste full output as comment or into INBOX.md.

Step 2 — For each .git directory found, check its remote:
  cd <repo_folder> && git remote -v
  Record: folder path + remote URL for each.

Step 3 — Cross-reference against the three repos being moved:
  - https://github.com/mrmeman555/OpenClaw_Claude
  - https://github.com/mrmeman555/Claude-Cowork-Vault  (is Home_Lab_2026)
  - https://github.com/mrmeman555/ClaudeTest (if exists)
  Any Z: repo pointing to same remote = COLLISION. Flag it.

Step 4 — Determine target path structure on Z:
  e.g. Z:/ML_OS/Home_Lab_2026, Z:/ML_OS/OpenClaw_Claude, Z:/ML_OS/ClaudeTest
  Ask Mimir to confirm the target path before proceeding.

Step 5 — Report findings, wait for Mimir decision:
  - List any collisions
  - List any stale/orphaned repos
  - Propose what to do with each (delete, archive, keep)
  - Propose the final Z: target path structure
  DO NOT move anything yet. Just report.

## Done state
- Full list of existing git repos on Z: documented
- Collision check complete
- Mimir has confirmed target path structure on Z:
- t-674952 unblocked and ready to proceed

## What NOT to do
- Do not move any folders yet
- Do not delete anything on Z: without Mimir confirmation
- Do not modify any existing repos on Z:
